title: DOCKER Container Port Mapping Validator Fixer

statement.markdown: Implement a function that validates Docker container port mappings. The function should take an object of container names to their corresponding port mappings as input. Each port mapping should be an array of two integers representing the host port and the container port. The function should return `true` if all port mappings are valid, and `false` otherwise. A port mapping is considered valid if the host port and container port are both within the valid range (1-65535) and the host port is not already in use by another container.
statement.inputFormat: Object of container names to their port mappings
statement.outputFormat: Boolean indicating whether all port mappings are valid
statement.notes: The function should not consider the case where a container has multiple port mappings with the same host port but different container ports.

constraints.description: Number of port mappings per container

examples.explanation: The second port mapping for container2 is invalid because the host port 8080 is already in use by container1

hints.text: Start by initializing an empty set to keep track of the host ports in use
Iterate over the port mappings for each container and check if the host port is already in use

editorial.title: Editorial: Docker Container Port Mapping Validator Fixer
editorial.intuition: The key to solving this problem is to keep track of the host ports in use as we iterate over the port mappings
editorial.bruteForce: A brute-force approach would involve checking each port mapping individually and verifying that the host port is not in use
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution would involve using a set to keep track of the host ports in use
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves initializing the set of host ports in use, iterating over the port mappings, and checking for validity
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the solution can be proven by showing that it correctly identifies valid and invalid port mappings
editorial.dryRun: A dry run of the solution would involve iterating over the port mappings and checking for validity
commonMistakes: Failing to initialize the set of host ports in use
